config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas yang tidak boleh dikorbankan begitu saja atas nama kepentingan masyarakat, karena sejarah sering menunjukkan bahwa alasan “demi kepentingan umum” bisa dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai tanpa menghapus martabat, kebebasan, dan perlindungan dasar setiap orang. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun ketika hak individu dihormati, bukan dikalahkan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over individual rights as a blanket rule. From my American perspective, that kind of statement is too broad and too dangerous, because governments and majorities often use "the public good" as a shortcut to trample unpopular people or minority rights. At the same time, individual rights are not absolute in every situation, since laws against violence, fraud, and public health threats show that society can set limits when there is a real, concrete harm. The key is that any limit on individual rights should be narrow, justified, and heavily scrutinized, not assumed to be automatically acceptable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu kalau dijadikan aturan umum, karena di Indonesia sendiri alasan “demi masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan terhadap warga. Memang benar hak individu tidak absolut, seperti soal kekerasan, penipuan, atau keadaan darurat kesehatan, tetapi pembatasannya harus sangat jelas, proporsional, dan tidak boleh jadi alasan untuk menekan kelompok yang tidak populer. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat dan hak individu harus dipertemukan, bukan dipertentangkan seolah yang satu selalu harus menang. Kalau hak individu dikorbankan terlalu mudah, yang paling sering rugi justru rakyat biasa, bukan mereka yang bicara atas nama masyarakat.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the statement as a general principle, because in the United States we have seen too many examples of “society first” becoming a cover for abuse, censorship, and discrimination. The other side is right that rights are not absolute, but that is exactly why limits must be tied to specific harms and checked carefully, not granted a blanket priority over the individual. I also do not think “balancing” should mean quietly letting the majority override unpopular people whenever it is convenient. In my view, a free society is strongest when individual rights are the default and collective interests have to prove they deserve an exception.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Pernyataan itu tetap terlalu berbahaya kalau dijadikan prinsip umum, karena di Indonesia label “kepentingan masyarakat” sering dipakai untuk menekan warga yang lemah atau tidak populer. Saya tidak menolak bahwa ada situasi tertentu, seperti kekerasan, penipuan, atau darurat kesehatan, di mana pembatasan hak individu memang perlu, tetapi itu harus benar-benar terbatas, proporsional, dan bisa diuji. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang baik bukan yang otomatis menomorsatukan mayoritas, melainkan yang mampu melindungi hak orang per orang sambil tetap mengejar kepentingan bersama. Kalau tidak, yang disebut kepentingan masyarakat sering kali hanya menjadi alasan untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kuasa.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over individual rights as a general rule, because in the United States that idea has too often been used to excuse overreach by governments and majorities. The strongest point from the other side is that rights cannot be absolute in every case, since real harms like violence, fraud, and public health crises sometimes require limits. But those limits should be specific, narrow, and evidence-based, not a blank check to override unpopular people in the name of the common good. To me, a healthy society protects individual rights first and only restricts them when there is a clear, serious, and closely justified reason.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
